description: DIAGNOSTIC: Capture qwen3's REAL SSE response format. Use a benign prompt (NO sensitive keywords) so APF does NOT block. We need to see the exact SSE event format qwen3 uses to match our block template.

services.capture_instructions: BEFORE submitting prompt: Open DevTools (F12) → Network tab → check 'Preserve log'. Submit the benign prompt '오늘 날씨가 어때?'. Wait for the AI to respond fully. Then: (1) Find the chat/completions API request in Network tab, (2) Click on it and show the EventStream tab — copy ALL events (or first 5 events), (3) Show the Response tab — copy first 500 chars of response body, (4) Show Headers tab — copy response headers. We need: exact SSE data format, JSON field names, streaming chunk structure.
services.expected: Need to capture: (a) What fields are in each SSE data: line (id, object, created, model, choices, delta, role, content, finish_reason), (b) How many events before [DONE], (c) Response headers (Transfer-Encoding, Content-Type, etc)
services.notes: This is a non-blocked request to see what NORMAL qwen3 SSE looks like. #370 showed our template has ZERO parsed SSE events — our format is wrong.

notes: From #370 analysis: browser receives 200 OK + 401 bytes but EventStream shows 0 events. Need to match real qwen3 format exactly. Also check if qwen3 uses Transfer-Encoding: chunked (no Content-Length) in real responses.
